codeReview.summary: The test suite implementation is comprehensive and passes all 35 tests across 13 test suites. The worker correctly created tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.test.js with Node.js built-in test runner covering all specified areas: JSON/YAML appendix parsing, section ordering, duplicate ID detection, schema validation, referential integrity checks, and navigation validation. The worker also fixed a legitimate bug in extractAppendix() where JSON/YAML parse errors were being silently ignored when parsing failed (json/yaml null but parseError set would fall through to the other format check).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The schema.json pattern for screen IDs (^SCREEN-[A-Za-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$) allows lowercase-first-letter screen IDs (e.g., SCREEN-board) which violates SPEC.md's PascalCase requirement (SCREEN-<PascalCaseName>). The test correctly works around this by using SCREEN-1invalid (number-first) to test invalid IDs. This pre-existing schema/spec inconsistency is documented but unresolved.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tooling/ui-md-linter/schema.json line ~30: pattern ^SCREEN-[A-Za-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$ vs SPEC.md which requires PascalCase. samples/kanban/UI.md uses SCREEN-board (lowercase b).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tooling-worker skill documents test command as 'node --test tooling/ui-md-linter/test*.js' but the actual test file is named 'ui-md-lint.test.js'. The glob pattern test*.js would match ui-md-lint.test.js correctly, so this is not an error but the naming could be more consistent.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill says 'node --test tooling/ui-md-linter/test*.js' but file is tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.test.js

summary: Feature implementation passes review. Created comprehensive test suite (tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.test.js) with 35 tests covering JSON/YAML parsing, section ordering, duplicate ID detection, schema validation, referential integrity, and navigation. Also fixed a bug in extractAppendix() where parse errors were silently ignored. All tests pass. The pre-existing schema/spec inconsistency (lowercase-first screen IDs allowed by schema but not by spec) was appropriately documented as non-blocking.
